methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Stratek Global is a growth-stage nuclear energy company founded by Kelvin Kemm. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 11 comparable entities: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. The nuclear energy sector contains only 8 comparable private companies, but filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier cohorts yields fewer than three datapoints in any single bucket. A median derived from the full 8-company set would obscure meaningful stage and scale differences and risk substantial misstatement.

Without a sufficiently narrow peer set meeting minimum statistical rigor, traditional multiples-based triangulation cannot be reliably executed here. A complete valuation narrative would require either: (1) expansion to alternative methodologies (discounted cash flow, sum-of-the-parts), (2) acquisition of additional comparable transaction or financing data within the growth-stage nuclear subsector, or (3) acceptance of a wider peer band with explicit caveats on applicability. The current dataset supports identification of the relevant peer universe but does not yield a defensible valuation range through peer multiples alone.
